meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Man charged with attempted murder, released after allegedly forcing toddler into crocodile enclosure at zoo
items.summary: A man was charged with attempted murder after allegedly forcing a 3-year-old boy into a crocodile enclosure at a zoo in Huntingdon, England.

items.title: Two-train crash leaves at least 1 dead, 89 injured as emergency crews rush to chaotic scene
items.summary: Two trains crashed into each other near Bedford, England, with an ambulance service confirming at least 1 death and over 80 people injured.

items.title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces potential leadership challenge from newly-elected Andy Burnham
items.summary: Labour's Andy Burnham won a special election on Friday and gained a seat in Parliament. He has openly spoken about challenging Keir Starmer as leader.

items.title: Trump's $300B Iran investment fund may be 'close to impossible' due to IRGC sanctions law, expert warns
items.summary: Trump's $300B Iran investment fund faces legal roadblocks as expert warns IRGC-controlled construction sector creates sanctions risks under existing law.

items.title: From bear hugs to handshakes: How India lost its edge with Trump while Pakistan quietly gained ground
items.summary: Amid India's push to isolate Pakistan, shifting geopolitics have elevated Islamabad's diplomatic profile. President Trump's meeting with Modi suggests New Delhi is looking to regain momentum.

items.title: Iran hardliner behind US deal warns Tehran won’t honor agreement if Trump fails to deliver
items.summary: Iran’s hardline parliament speaker and key negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned that Tehran would not honor its commitments under a newly signed memorandum with the U.S.

items.title: 'Pure hell' in Moscow as Ukrainian drones strike major refinery supplying capital's fuel market
items.summary: Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks on Moscow, striking a major oil refinery and sending thick black smoke over parts of the Russian capital.

items.title: Finland's parliament votes to lift decades-old ban on nuclear weapons in historic NATO defense shift
items.summary: Finland's parliament votes to lift a decades-old nuclear weapons ban in a historic defense policy shift to align more closely with NATO deterrence.

items.title: Colombian military neutralizes five terrorists accused in bus bombing that killed 20, injured 45 civilians
items.summary: Colombian military forces neutralized five members of the militant group allegedly responsible for the bus bombing that killed 20 civilians in Cauca.

items.title: Israel launches 'Biblical Highway' amid campaign to erase Jewish history
items.summary: Israel's government brands Route 60 as the Biblical Highway, aiming to strengthen heritage tourism and the nation's identity as the land of the Bible.

items.title: Elon Musk demands prison for politicians who 'turned a blind eye' to grooming gangs as new report released
items.summary: Elon Musk amplified a citizen-funded report accusing the British state of failing to protect children from organized grooming gang exploitation across England.

items.title: 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope
items.summary: A 21-year-old woman died after instructors launched her from a bridge without attaching safety ropes during a rope-jumping event near São Paulo, Brazil.

items.title: Russia linked to arson attacks on properties connected to UK PM Keir Starmer, police say
items.summary: Russia allegedly linked to arson attacks on U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's properties, with a reported diplomat accused of recruiting Ukrainian nationals.

items.title: Netanyahu's Israel grapples with Trump-Iran deal as details remain unclear
items.summary: Defense Minister Katz says IDF will remain in Lebanon security zones as Israeli leaders debate Trump-Iran deal's impact on national security interests.

items.title: Iran’s regime spins nuclear and Strait of Hormuz deal with Trump as victory over US, Israel
items.summary: Trump announces U.S.-Iran MOU to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and address Iran's nuclear program while Iranian media claims victory over Washington.

items.title: Anti-G7 protest turns violent as demonstrators torch Tesla and smash UN office windows
items.summary: Protesters set a Tesla vehicle on fire and smashed windows at a United Nations office in Geneva as they marched against an upcoming Group of Seven summit.

items.title: Khamenei's 'target-rich' funeral is Iran’s biggest security gamble, sends message to US: expert
items.summary: Iran's July funeral for Khamenei is a high-stakes bet that any peace deal with the U.S. will hold, creating a target-rich event, expert warns.

items.title: Israel fears Trump weary of ‘highly suspicious’ Netanyahu and could 'flip' amid Iran deal: analyst
items.summary: An analyst says fears are growing in Jerusalem that President Trump could flip on Netanyahu amid U.S.-Iran deal tensions, with strikes in Beirut complicating talks.

items.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
items.summary: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

items.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
items.summary: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

items.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
items.summary: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

items.title: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report
items.summary: Mexican authorities found a decomposing corpse with signs of violence near Tijuana's Caliente Stadium, where Iran's team trains for the World Cup.

items.title: UNRWA fires 70 Gaza staffers amid allegations of Hamas ties, says terminations not admission of guilt
items.summary: The UNRWA fired 70 Gaza staff members amid long-standing Israeli claims the agency collaborates with Hamas, though it says dismissals are not an admission.

items.title: Woman airlifted to hospital with serious injuries after shark attack at popular Sydney beach
items.summary: A 30-year-old woman suffered serious injuries to her arm and leg after a shark attack at Sydney's Coogee Beach, the latest in a string of incidents.

items.title: Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump’s Iran pressure campaign after regional trip: ‘Zero daylight’
items.summary: Ambassador Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump's blockade and economic pressure campaign against Iran, with UAE and Bahrain validating U.S. strategy.
